persona.description: An experienced shopper who already trusts familiar brands and compares details quickly.

behavior_description: 0-2s: The shopper examines the product display. 2-5s: The shopper takes out a phone and looks at it while standing next to the product. 5-8s: The shopper alternates between the phone screen and the product, as if comparing information. 8-10s: The shopper keeps the phone in hand and continues reviewing the product.

behavior_timeline.event: The shopper examines the product display.
The shopper takes out a phone and looks at it while standing next to the product.
The shopper alternates between the phone screen and the product, as if comparing information.
The shopper keeps the phone in hand and continues reviewing the product.

kling_prompt_sections.camera: 10-second fixed in-store surveillance-style shot, slightly elevated oblique angle; customer body trajectory, dwell time, hand movement, product display, and counter area visible; face detail is helpful but not required; one primary shopper, natural movement, no subtitles, no voiceover, no brand logos.
kling_prompt_sections.scene: a premium apparel store with a tidy fitting area nearby; visible product setup: two jackets on a rack beside a mirror and discreet price tags.

behavior_timeline.event: The shopper examines the product display.
The shopper takes out a phone and looks at it while standing next to the product.
The shopper alternates between the phone screen and the product, as if comparing information.
The shopper keeps the phone in hand and continues reviewing the product.

kling_prompt_sections.negative: no extreme overhead shot, no tiny distant customer, no occluded product area, no extra shoppers, no completed purchase, no salesperson speech, no exaggerated acting, no floating labels, no hidden-category labels, no intervention labels, no scoring text, no UI overlays.

kling_prompt:
Camera: 10-second fixed in-store surveillance-style shot, slightly elevated oblique angle; customer body trajectory, dwell time, hand movement, product display, and counter area visible; face detail is helpful but not required; one primary shopper, natural movement, no subtitles, no voiceover, no brand logos.
Scene: a premium apparel store with a tidy fitting area nearby; visible product setup: two jackets on a rack beside a mirror and discreet price tags.
Customer behavior style: The shopper stays near the display and examines details with visible curiosity.
Behavior timeline:
- 0-2s: The shopper examines the product display.
- 2-5s: The shopper takes out a phone and looks at it while standing next to the product.
- 5-8s: The shopper alternates between the phone screen and the product, as if comparing information.
- 8-10s: The shopper keeps the phone in hand and continues reviewing the product.
Negative constraints: no extreme overhead shot, no tiny distant customer, no occluded product area, no extra shoppers, no completed purchase, no salesperson speech, no exaggerated acting, no floating labels, no hidden-category labels, no intervention labels, no scoring text, no UI overlays.
